name: multi-agent-brainstorming
description: Use this skill when a design or idea requires higher confidence, risk reduction, or formal review. This skill orchestrates a structured, sequential multi-agent design review where each agent has a strict, non-overlapping role. It prevents blind spots, false confidence, and premature convergence.
capabilities: Orchestrate a sequential design review with five distinct agent roles (Designer, Skeptic, Constraint Guardian, User Advocate, Integrator)
Prevent idea swarm chaos and false consensus through enforced role constraints
Identify hidden assumptions and failure modes before implementation
Maintain a mandatory Decision Log with all objections and resolutions
Enforce hard exit criteria to ensure review completeness
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate multi-agent design review framework. Detected patterns are markdown formatting (backticks for skill references) and design review terminology, not actual security threats. No malicious code, commands, or cryptographic algorithms present.
valueStatement: Single-agent designs often miss hidden assumptions and failure modes. This skill runs a structured, sequential review with five specialized agents to validate designs before implementation.
limitations: Does not execute code or perform actual design implementation
Requires Claude or Codex to manage multiple agent interactions
Review quality depends on the clarity of the initial design request
Cannot validate designs against external technical constraints without explicit input

useCases.targetUser: Software architects and senior developers building critical systems
useCases.title: Critical System Design Validation
useCases.description: Validate architectural decisions for production systems where failure has significant consequences.

useCases.targetUser: Product managers and team leads validating feature designs
useCases.title: Feature Design Peer Review
useCases.description: Review new feature proposals before development begins to catch edge cases and usability issues.

useCases.targetUser: Technical writers and team leads needing audit trails for decisions
useCases.title: Technical Decision Documentation
useCases.description: Create a structured Decision Log that documents all design choices and their rationale.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Design Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the multi-agent-brainstorming skill to review this design: [describe your design idea]. Run the full review process and produce a complete Decision Log.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have a design concept and want structured validation

promptTemplates.title: Security-Focused Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the multi-agent-brainstorming skill to review [describe design]. Focus the Constraint Guardian agent specifically on security and privacy aspects.
promptTemplates.scenario: When security is a primary concern for the design

promptTemplates.title: User Experience Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the multi-agent-brainstorming skill to review [describe design]. Emphasize the User Advocate agent to evaluate user experience and usability.
promptTemplates.scenario: When the design directly affects end-user interactions

promptTemplates.title: Quick Feasibility Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the multi-agent-brainstorming skill for a rapid review of [describe design]. Focus on identifying showstoppers and major risks only.
promptTemplates.scenario: When time is limited and you need a quick sanity check

bestPractices: Provide clear, specific design requirements to the Primary Designer before starting review
Ensure all five agent roles are invoked in sequence for comprehensive coverage
Complete the Decision Log for every design review as mandatory documentation
antiPatterns: Skipping the Skeptic agent - challenging assumptions is critical for finding hidden flaws
Allowing the Primary Designer to self-approve without Integrator review
Exiting the review process before all exit criteria are met

faq.question: What is the difference between multi-agent-brainstorming and regular brainstorming?
faq.answer: Regular brainstorming is parallel and creative. Multi-agent-brainstorming is sequential and critical. It enforces structured review with specific agent roles to find flaws, not generate ideas.

faq.question: Can I skip some agent roles to save time?
faq.answer: No. Each agent role serves a specific purpose. Skipping roles violates the skill's exit criteria and reduces review quality.

faq.question: What happens if an objection cannot be resolved?
faq.answer: The Integrator must explicitly reject the objection with rationale, documenting it in the Decision Log. The design cannot proceed without this explicit rejection.

faq.question: How do I know when the review is complete?
faq.answer: The review is complete when: Understanding Lock is done, all reviewers invoked, all objections resolved or rejected, Decision Log complete, and Arbiter declares acceptable.

faq.question: Can this skill work with any Claude or Codex setup?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with any Claude, Codex, or Claude Code environment that can manage multi-turn conversations with defined agent roles.

faq.question: What if I need to reopen a completed design decision?
faq.answer: The Integrator must provide explicit cause for reopening. Without cause, locked decisions remain final per the skill's non-negotiable rules.
